questions.text: Electrostatic potential at a point is defined as the work done in bringing a unit positive charge from ________ to that point.
questions.explanation: Infinity is taken as the reference point where potential is assumed to be zero.

questions.text: The SI unit of electrostatic potential is:
questions.explanation: 1 Volt = 1 Joule / 1 Coulomb.

questions.text: The electrostatic potential due to a point charge 'q' at a distance 'r' varies as:
questions.explanation: Potential V = (1 / 4πε₀) * (q/r).

questions.text: The work done in moving a charge over an equipotential surface is:
questions.explanation: Work done W = q(V2 - V1). Since V2 = V1 on an equipotential surface, W = 0.

questions.text: Electric field is always ________ to the equipotential surface at every point.
questions.explanation: If there were a tangential component, work would be done moving a charge along the surface, contradicting the definition of equipotential.

questions.text: For a uniform electric field along the x-axis, the equipotential surfaces are planes parallel to:
questions.explanation: Equipotential surfaces are perpendicular to the field lines.

questions.text: The electrostatic potential at any point on the axial line of an electric dipole is:
questions.explanation: V = (1 / 4πε₀) * (p / r^2) for large distances r.

questions.text: The electrostatic potential at any point on the equatorial plane of an electric dipole is:
questions.explanation: On the equatorial line, the potential due to +q and -q charges cancels out exactly.

questions.text: Capacitance of a conductor depends on:
questions.explanation: Capacitance C = Q/V is a property determined by the physical configuration and environment.

questions.text: The SI unit of capacitance is:
questions.explanation: 1 Farad = 1 Coulomb / 1 Volt.

questions.text: The capacitance of a parallel plate capacitor is given by C =
questions.explanation: C is directly proportional to area A and inversely proportional to separation d.

questions.text: When a dielectric slab is inserted between the plates of a capacitor, its capacitance:
questions.explanation: The dielectric reduces the effective electric field, decreasing potential and thus increasing C (since C = Q/V).

questions.text: The energy stored in a capacitor of capacitance C and potential V is:
In a series combination of n capacitors, which quantity remains the same for each?
questions.explanation: In series, the same charge Q flows through every capacitor.

questions.text: In a parallel combination of capacitors, which quantity remains the same for each?
questions.explanation: Capacitors in parallel are connected across the same two points, ensuring identical V.

questions.text: Dielectric constant K is defined as the ratio of:
options: Capacitance with dielectric to capacitance in vacuum
Field in vacuum to field in dielectric
Permittivity of medium to permittivity of vacuum
questions.explanation: K is a dimensionless factor representing the factor by which the field is reduced or capacitance increased.

questions.text: The potential energy of a system of two point charges q1 and q2 separated by distance r is:
questions.explanation: Work done in bringing q2 into the potential field of q1.

questions.text: A polar molecule is one in which the center of positive and negative charges:
questions.explanation: Polar molecules (like H2O) have a permanent dipole moment.

questions.text: The maximum electric field that a dielectric medium can withstand without break-down is called its:
questions.explanation: It is measured in V/m. Above this limit, the insulating property fails.

questions.text: The relation between electric field E and potential V is:
questions.explanation: The electric field is the negative gradient of the electrostatic potential.

questions.text: Total capacitance of three capacitors C1, C2, C3 in parallel is:
questions.explanation: Parallel combination adds the storage capacities directly.

questions.text: Potential energy of a dipole in a uniform electric field is minimum when θ is:
questions.explanation: U = -pE cos θ. At θ=0, U = -pE (minimum/stable equilibrium).

questions.text: A capacitor of 4μF is charged to 50V. The energy stored is:
Non-polar molecules (like O2, H2) have a permanent dipole moment equal to:
questions.explanation: Their centers of positive and negative charges coincide in the absence of an external field.

questions.text: If two capacitors of 6μF each are connected in series, the equivalent capacitance is:
One Electron Volt (eV) is equal to:
questions.explanation: It is the kinetic energy gained by an electron when accelerated through a potential difference of 1 Volt.

questions.text: The capacitance of a spherical conductor of radius R is:
questions.explanation: Derived from V = Q / 4πε₀R and C = Q/V.

questions.text: Sharp pointed ends of a conductor have ________ surface charge density.
questions.explanation: Charge density σ is inversely proportional to the radius of curvature.

questions.text: Work done in moving a charge from one point to another in an electric field depends on:
questions.explanation: Electrostatic force is a conservative force, so work is path-independent.

questions.text: The polarization of a dielectric is proportional to:
questions.explanation: P = χₑ E, where χₑ is electrical susceptibility.

questions.text: What happens to the potential at a point if the distance from the point charge is doubled?
questions.explanation: V ∝ 1/r. If r becomes 2r, V becomes V/2.

questions.text: Earth is considered to be a conductor at ________ potential.
questions.explanation: Earth is so large that its potential doesn't change significantly when charge is added/removed; thus it's used as a zero reference.

questions.text: When two capacitors are joined in parallel, they share the:
questions.explanation: Parallel components always share the same potential difference.

questions.text: A dielectric ________ the electric field within it.
questions.explanation: Induced charges create an internal field opposite to the external field, reducing the net field.

questions.text: Potential energy of a dipole is maximum (unstable equilibrium) when θ is:
questions.explanation: At 180°, cos θ = -1, so U = -pE(-1) = +pE.

questions.text: Which of the following is a unit of energy density of electric field?
questions.explanation: Energy density is energy per unit volume.

questions.text: If distance between plates of a parallel plate capacitor is halved, capacitance becomes:
questions.explanation: C ∝ 1/d. If d becomes d/2, C becomes 2C.

questions.text: Electric potential is a:
questions.explanation: Potential is defined by work/charge, both of which are scalars.

questions.text: Equipotential surfaces due to a single point charge are:
questions.explanation: Since V = kQ/r, all points at the same distance r (forming a sphere) have the same potential.

questions.text: The formula for energy density 'u' in a capacitor is:
questions.explanation: u represents energy stored per unit volume in the electric field between plates.
